required_before_run: commit generated docs/design and tasks/design artifacts to the referenced branch main
push the committed branch to origin before spec-executor clones the remote repo

verify_runtime.command_strategy: try py -3, then python3, then python for tasks/design/validate_design_doc_quality.py with --planning tasks/design/design-planning.json

source_inventory.used_for: product requirements, QueryPlan product design, acceptance criteria, metrics, risks, release gates
architecture boundaries, module responsibilities, runtime flow, ADRs, detailed-design handoff points
project constitution, Rust CLI constraint, provider/channel/OpenClaw/retry rules, no-gstack rule
repository entrypoint and evidence that no Cargo project exists yet

prd_hld_interpretation.current_phase: detailed design planning for Rust CLI MVP
prd_hld_interpretation.product_scope: QueryPlan-driven image search, candidate validation/evaluation, batched retrieval, image acceptance, retry or limited delivery, and delivery package generation
prd_hld_interpretation.architecture_summary: single-process local Rust CLI with task orchestration core, readiness self-check, pluggable BaseProvider, pluggable BaseRetrievalChannel, OpenClaw evaluation port, two quality gates, policy guardrails, evidence metrics, and delivery builder
hard_boundaries: no Web UI, SaaS, service queue, account system, or image editing/generation scope
production subjective evaluation must use OpenClaw and must not be replaced by mock or fixture
retrieval fallback must not bypass login, paywall, access control, site authorization, or paid-channel enablement boundaries
fourth retrieval channel is unresolved and must not be invented in detailed design

default real search provider, built-in provider list, OpenClaw protocol, authorization blocking details, paid channel enablement, robots/site-rule policy, and maximum QueryPlan count remain open decisions
detailed_design_obligations: define Rust crate/module boundaries and domain types for the CLI MVP
design QueryPlan parsing, CLI command boundary, defaulting, derived planning values, and input rejection
design BaseProvider contract, search scheduling, provider readiness, source attribution, candidate shortage handling, and weighted random behavior
design candidate mechanical validation, structured candidate evaluation boundary, OpenClaw candidate evaluation, and candidate decision normalization
design BaseRetrievalChannel contract, retrieval batch planning, fallback control, channel tiers, short batch handling, retrieval result classification, and policy-owned fallback blocking handoff
design image mechanical acceptance, OpenClaw image acceptance, task state machine, attempt accounting, full delivery termination, execution blocking, retry, and limited delivery
design delivery package directory/file layout, machine-readable status contract, policy guardrails, observability, diagnostics, verification plan, and security boundaries
design readiness self-check aggregation after provider/channel/candidate OpenClaw/image OpenClaw/policy boundaries are detailed

in_scope: documentation-only detailed design tasks under docs/design
spec-executor 2.0 pointer specs for each task
out_of_scope: new product requirements not already present in PRD/HLD/AGENTS

rust_stack.repo_state: no Cargo.toml or Rust source tree exists yet
rust_stack.allowed_design_level: detailed design may specify planned crate/module/type/trait/error/state/async boundaries, but must not implement them
blocked_areas: specific Rust libraries, HTTP clients, async runtime, serialization crates, and CLI parser crates are not fixed by PRD/HLD and must be recorded as design decisions or open options rather than invented dependencies

checks: HLD details design handoff points identified
findings: sources are sufficient for detailed-design planning with open decisions recorded as risks
eight detailed design tasks plus one final acceptance task cover current-phase obligations

planning_review_stages.stage: review each task for safe execution
findings: each task can be executed as a bounded documentation-only design task
dependency direction follows design information flow from baseline through provider/channel/evaluation/policy details to readiness and acceptance
generated artifacts are local and require commit/push before remote spec-executor consumption

checks: acceptance depends on all detailed design tasks
findings: TASK-009 reviews the completed design document set before implementation planning

tasks.title: Rust Implementation Baseline And Domain Model Design
outputs: overall Rust crate and module boundary design
domain type and state boundary design
cross-task handoff map for docs/design
allowed_scope: design crate/module boundaries, domain entities, error families, state ownership, async/concurrency stance, and design handoffs
forbidden_scope: Do not implement code
do not create Cargo.toml
do not select unsupported third-party Rust libraries as committed facts
acceptance: document covers all required rust-implementation-design.md sections
cites PRD/HLD/AGENTS
records open technology decisions without inventing product facts
stop_conditions: stop if Rust CLI scope or single-process architecture becomes contradictory
stop if a required library/protocol choice cannot be derived from PRD/HLD

tasks.title: QueryPlan CLI And Input Planning Detailed Design
outputs: QueryPlan input contract design
CLI command boundary design
defaulting and derived planning value design
input rejection diagnostics design
allowed_scope: design CLI input normalization, QueryPlan validation/defaults, derived candidate/batch/retry planning values, input rejection diagnostics, and verification plan
forbidden_scope: Do not implement code
do not define production CLI parser library as a fact
do not change product defaults
acceptance: covers FR-001, FR-002, AC-001, AC-002
distinguishes input rejection from delivery results
stop_conditions: stop if required QueryPlan fields or CLI syntax would require new product decisions

tasks.title: BaseProvider Search Scheduler Detailed Design
outputs: BaseProvider trait and adapter boundary design
weighted random scheduling design
provider readiness and candidate shortage design
candidate source attribution design
allowed_scope: design provider contract, scheduler state, failure normalization, weighted random behavior, and verification strategy
forbidden_scope: Do not implement code
do not pick a default real provider
do not define concrete external provider HTTP protocol
acceptance: covers FR-003, FR-004, AC-003, AC-004, MET-002
keeps BaseProvider aligned with BaseSearchProvider constitutional term
stop_conditions: stop if default provider selection or built-in provider list becomes necessary

tasks.title: Candidate Quality Gate And OpenClaw Candidate Evaluation Detailed Design
outputs: candidate mechanical validation design
structured candidate evaluation boundary design
OpenClaw candidate evaluation result normalization design
candidate priority and rejection evidence design
allowed_scope: design blocking/reference metric categories, candidate decision states, OpenClaw availability behavior, and candidate evidence handoff
forbidden_scope: Do not implement code
do not define OpenClaw wire protocol
do not invent algorithm thresholds
acceptance: covers FR-005, FR-011, AC-005, AC-011, MET-004, MET-006
keeps uncertain candidate conclusions out of retrieval batches
stop_conditions: stop if OpenClaw production responsibility or skill execution model must be finalized first

tasks.title: BaseRetrievalChannel Batch And Fallback Detailed Design
inputs: TASK-002 required count and batch target
outputs: BaseRetrievalChannel trait and adapter boundary design
retrieval batch planning design
fallback policy handoff design including local rejection versus task-level execution blocking
short batch and retrieval failure classification design
allowed_scope: design channel tier model, channel capability/failure outputs, batch selection, fallback orchestration handoff, local rejection versus task-level blocking, and paid/disabled channel boundaries
forbidden_scope: Do not implement code
do not invent a fourth channel
do not bypass access or authorization limits
acceptance: covers FR-006, FR-007, AC-006, AC-007, MET-005
records fourth-channel uncertainty as an open decision
stop_conditions: stop if paid channel enablement or fourth channel becomes required to finish the design

tasks.title: Image Acceptance And Task Orchestrator State Machine Detailed Design
outputs: image mechanical acceptance design
OpenClaw image evaluation normalization design
Task Orchestrator state machine design
attempt counting and delivery decision design
allowed_scope: design image acceptance states, full delivery termination, execution blocking, retry, limited delivery, and no-real-image batch behavior
forbidden_scope: Do not implement code
do not change initial attempt plus 3 retries rule
do not let mock/fixture count as production acceptance
acceptance: distinguishes retry_count and full_attempt_count
stop_conditions: stop if OpenClaw image evaluation responsibility cannot be represented without unsupported protocol details

tasks.title: Delivery Package Policy Observability And Verification Detailed Design
inputs: TASK-004 candidate quality and OpenClaw events
TASK-005 retrieval channel and fallback events
TASK-006 final task states and evidence
outputs: delivery package directory and file layout design
machine-readable delivery status and manifest contract design
policy and guardrail decision design
diagnostics and observability event source design
fixture and real-service verification plan design
allowed_scope: design delivery package contents, directory/file organization, manifest/status files, machine-readable boundary, sensitive data exclusion, metric event sources, release verification and rollback evidence
forbidden_scope: Do not implement code
do not choose runtime serialization libraries as committed facts
do not mark unknown authorization as commercial-safe
acceptance: covers FR-010, FR-013, NFR-001 through NFR-006, MET-001 through MET-006, AC-010, AC-013
explicitly maps MET-001 through MET-006 to event sources from provider, candidate, retrieval, OpenClaw, orchestrator, and delivery boundaries
defines detailed delivery package layout and machine-readable contract within PRD/HLD scope
stop_conditions: stop if authorization grouping, robots policy, maximum QueryPlan count, or exact runtime serialization library must be decided before design can proceed

tasks.title: Readiness Self-check Integration Detailed Design
inputs: TASK-004 OpenClaw candidate evaluation availability model
TASK-005 retrieval channel readiness and enablement model
TASK-006 OpenClaw image evaluation availability and execution-blocking model
TASK-007 policy guardrails and diagnostics model
outputs: Self-check Report detailed design
readiness aggregation control flow design
provider/channel/candidate OpenClaw/image OpenClaw/policy readiness input design
self-check diagnostics and non-delivery boundary design
allowed_scope: design readiness self-check command behavior, aggregation of QueryPlan/provider/channel/candidate OpenClaw/image OpenClaw/policy readiness, user-facing diagnostics, automation-readable check status, and verification plan
forbidden_scope: Do not implement code
do not perform search/retrieval/production subjective evaluation in self-check
do not generate a delivery package from self-check
acceptance: covers FR-012 and AC-012
depends on detailed provider/channel/candidate OpenClaw/image OpenClaw/policy readiness contracts rather than inventing them
keeps self-check separate from formal delivery results
stop_conditions: stop if candidate OpenClaw readiness, image OpenClaw readiness, provider readiness, retrieval channel enablement, or policy blocker semantics cannot be represented from upstream design documents

tasks.title: Detailed Design Acceptance Review
outputs: final detailed design acceptance report under docs/design
cross-document consistency verdict
blocker and residual risk list
allowed_scope: review completed docs/design deliverables for traceability, consistency, detailed design quality, and readiness for development planning
forbidden_scope: Do not implement code
do not modify production files
do not introduce new design scope outside the planned documents
acceptance: mentions every detailed design task
inventories every design document
records pass/fail/blocked verdict and required fixes
stop_conditions: block if any planned detailed design document is missing or contradicts PRD/HLD

edges.reason: QueryPlan and CLI input planning depend on shared Rust/domain baseline.
Provider trait and scheduler types depend on module and error baseline.
Search target and defaults derive from QueryPlan planning.
Quality gate types and decisions depend on shared domain and error model.
Candidate evaluation needs QueryPlan semantics, quality, and content constraints.
Candidate gate consumes normalized search candidates and source attribution.
Retrieval channel trait and batch types depend on shared Rust baseline.
Batch target uses QueryPlan required count.
Retrieval batch planner consumes only可抓取候选序列.
State machine and acceptance decisions depend on shared domain and error model.
Retry limits and default planning come from QueryPlan planning.
Image acceptance must align with candidate gate and OpenClaw normalization semantics.
Image acceptance and orchestrator consume retrieval results and fallback outcomes.
Delivery, policy, and observability types use shared baseline.
Output preference affects delivery and automation boundaries.
Observability and delivery evidence need provider usage, candidate counts, and source attribution.
Observability and rejection explanations need candidate gate and OpenClaw candidate event sources.
Observability and delivery evidence need retrieval channel and fallback event sources.
Delivery package consumes final task states, accepted images, failures, and evidence.
Readiness self-check uses shared domain and result boundary.
Self-check validates QueryPlan legality and default explainability.
Self-check needs BaseProvider readiness and provider configuration boundary.
Self-check needs candidate OpenClaw evaluation availability and skill executability model.
Self-check needs image OpenClaw evaluation availability and skill executability model before formal task execution.
Self-check needs retrieval channel readiness and enablement boundary.
Self-check aggregates policy blockers and user-facing diagnostics.
Final acceptance must review TASK-001 design output.
Final acceptance must review TASK-002 design output.
Final acceptance must review TASK-003 design output.
Final acceptance must review TASK-004 design output.
Final acceptance must review TASK-005 design output.
Final acceptance must review TASK-006 design output.
Final acceptance must review TASK-007 design output.
Final acceptance must review TASK-008 design output.

parallel_groups.reason: Provider design follows QueryPlan derived target and feeds candidate quality and readiness.
Readiness self-check runs after all upstream readiness inputs are detailed and before final acceptance.

coverage_matrix.coverage: QueryPlan input, defaults, derived planning values, CLI boundary, and input rejection detailed design
candidate target, BaseProvider, provider scheduling, readiness, attribution, and provider-derived observability event sources detailed design
candidate mechanical validation, OpenClaw candidate evaluation, candidate normalization, rejection reason metrics, and OpenClaw pass-rate event sources detailed design
batch retrieval, BaseRetrievalChannel, channel fallback, short batch, and channel effectiveness event sources detailed design
image acceptance, OpenClaw image evaluation, state machine, retry, full delivery, execution blocking, limited delivery, task result distribution, and accepted image achievement detailed design
delivery package directory/file layout, machine-readable manifest/status contract, policy guardrails, security, diagnostics, release verification, and automation consumption detailed design
readiness self-check aggregation after provider/channel/candidate OpenClaw/image OpenClaw/policy readiness contracts are detailed

planning_gate_report.evidence: tasks and coverage matrix cite PRD/HLD/AGENTS refs
Rust CLI stack fixed; no Cargo skeleton yet recorded
runtime and state machine obligations assigned
QueryPlan, candidates, retrieval results, accepted images, readiness inputs, delivery evidence, and metric event sources assigned
ADRs mapped to detailed design tasks
cycle_check pass and semantic edges recorded
parallel groups are limited and dependency-safe
spec.yaml pointer metadata planned for every task
generated local artifacts require commit/push before remote spec-executor use
TASK-009 depends on all detailed design tasks

external_dependencies.dependency: OpenClaw production evaluation usage and responsibility boundary
external_dependencies.impact: blocks production subjective evaluation details beyond port boundary
blocks concrete default adapter and real-service validation matrix
blocks exact paid-channel runtime enablement design

external_dependencies.dependency: authorization blocking details and robots/site-rule policy
external_dependencies.impact: blocks exact policy outcomes for some sources

open_questions: Does a fourth retrieval channel exist, or was the constitution wording a mismatch with the three listed categories?
Should single QueryPlan maximum delivery count be constrained in MVP?
Should delivery results be grouped by authorization risk in MVP or only carry risk labels?
Which real image search provider is the first default provider for local real-service validation?
